title: Tick Control Great Kills
metaDescription: Professional tick control in Great Kills, Staten Island. Protect your family and pets from tick-borne diseases. Same-day service available. Call now: $125-$325.
intro: Great Kills residents face unique tick challenges due to the neighborhood's proximity to Great Kills Park and abundant green spaces along the shoreline. Our professional tick control services protect your family and pets from Lyme disease and other tick-borne illnesses common in Staten Island's coastal communities. We provide comprehensive tick treatment for residential properties throughout Great Kills, from the waterfront homes near Raritan Bay to the inland neighborhoods surrounding Hylan Boulevard.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has served Great Kills homeowners for years with proven tick elimination methods tailored to Staten Island's unique ecosystem. Our licensed technicians understand the coastal environment and use EPA-approved treatments that are safe for children, pets, and the local wildlife near Great Kills Park.
localContext: Great Kills' abundance of parks, wooded areas, and proximity to the beach create ideal tick habitats, especially around Great Kills Harbor and the residential streets near Buffalo Street and Hylan Boulevard. Properties backing onto Great Kills Park or featuring landscaped yards are particularly susceptible to tick infestations during warmer months.

faqs.question: Why is tick control important in Great Kills?
faqs.answer: Great Kills' location near Great Kills Park and wooded coastal areas makes it a hotspot for deer ticks and other species that carry Lyme disease and other illnesses. Regular tick control protects your family and pets, especially if you enjoy outdoor activities near the park or waterfront areas.

faqs.question: How much does tick control cost in Great Kills?
faqs.answer: Our tick control services in Great Kills range from $125 to $325, depending on your property size and the extent of treatment needed. We offer free inspections and customized treatment plans for homes throughout the neighborhood.

faqs.question: When is tick season in Great Kills, Staten Island?
faqs.answer: Ticks are most active in Great Kills from April through October, with peak activity in spring and fall. However, mild winters near the coast can extend tick season, so year-round prevention is recommended for properties near Great Kills Park and wooded areas.

faqs.question: Are your tick treatments safe for pets and children?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved tick control products that are safe for children and pets once dry. Our technicians will provide specific instructions after treatment, typically recommending that kids and pets stay off treated areas for 2-4 hours until completely dry.
